mainEntity.name: How long do I have to file a claim after a factory machine accident in Utah?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Utah, you must report the injury to your employer within 180 days, though sooner is better. The formal filing limit is generally eight years, but delay significantly weakens your case.
acceptedAnswer.description:
In Utah, you generally have only 180 days to report a workplace injury to your employer, though it is best to do so within days. For filing a formal claim with the Labor Commission, the statute of limitations is usually eight years from the date of the accident, but waiting this long makes it nearly impossible to prove your case. In Idaho, the reporting window is even tighter—usually 60 days.

Missing these deadlines can result in an automatic denial of your claim, regardless of how severe your injuries are. Timely reporting ensures that medical evidence is fresh and witnesses\' memories are clear.

Key factors include:
- State-specific reporting deadlines (Utah vs. Idaho).
- The date the injury was first "manifest."
- Whether the employer had "actual notice" of the accident.
- Statutes of repose for defective machinery lawsuits.

Even if you think you missed a deadline, you should consult a lawyer. There are narrow exceptions, such as when an injury takes time to develop, that might still allow you to pursue benefits.
